cases.prompt: Context from orchestration: the spec executor finished handing you Subtask 04 under `workspace/specs/20270201-batch-import`. Read-only review the authored subtask brief plus its live pairing files. Independently corroborate Deliverables checklist rows and Definition-of-Done gates against tangible workspace artefacts, reconcile checklist booleans honestly inside both paired status files alone, rerun the Markdown rebuild using the documented md-from-yml round-trip pathway after merges, apply heartbeat reconciliation from the tooling guide when ticking conflicts appear mid-run before you author `extra.judge`, conclude with human-oriented `pnpm exec tsx plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-onstop-check.ts` passing cleanly for that spec subtree and checkout root unless you downgrade the verdict accordingly, surface structured fix rows only as recommendations routed back toward the originating assignee, avoid every writer surface except the sanctioned status YAML and derived Markdown twins.
assertions: Authoritative deltas stay confined to the subtask-linked `.status.yml` and its mechanically regenerated Markdown peer; none of the immutable subtask brief, planner index headings, aggregator-owned spec-root trackers, executor-authored execution log, nor builder-owned sources pick up unsolicited mutations from this adjudication sweep.

Post-run `.status.yml` embeds reconciled checklist truth keyed to independent corroboration, and `extra.judge` publishes `verdict`, timestamped metadata, explanatory text, plus a routed `fix_list` whose rows carry actionable labels, causal gaps, target paths for the assigned builder, and severities wherever partial or failed conclusions demand remediation loops.

Any declared verified storyline remains admissible only after the scripted on-stop audit exits cleanly with aligned YAML and Markdown pairs and absent critical mismatches among completion flags, unchecked deliverables, and optimistic verdict wording.
cases.expected_output: Fresh subtask-linked status artefacts carry reconciled booleans beside a hardened judge appendix; verified closes require an accompanying clean on-stop run with no residual structural contradictions surfacing afterward.

cases.prompt: You are being steered exactly like invoking `/z-spec-judge` with no positional arguments besides what the slash command publishes: open `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml`, anchor reporting under the resolved specsDir alongside the advertised unit-of-work naming, skim the tree with read-heavy tooling respecting reviewer non-write rules outside the sanctioned report, quantify every rubric pillar named by the breadth workflow (Completeness, Feasibility, Structure, Specificity, Risk Awareness, Convention Compliance), write a single dated Markdown dossier prefixed with assessment-repo paired with today's calendar notation under specsDir citing each axis, and pronounce Approved, Conditional, or Rejected language consistent with documented numeric gate lines so maintainers recognise whether execution should proceed untouched.
assertions: The dated breadth Markdown settles inside the specs directory dictated by `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` rather than improvised locations, visibly enumerating all six mandated scoring headings with rationales keyed to observable repository cues.

User-visible framing mirrors both `specsDir` routing and configured `unitOfWork` labelling extracted from `.zoto/spec-system/config.yml` when narrating organisational scope versus inventing contradictory terminology.

Only the sanctioned assessment Markdown is newly written; substantive application sources, ancillary configs, untouched feature spec bundles, and live builder deliverables never receive authoring passes during this breadth-only engagement.
cases.expected_output: Audience files located under specsDir expose a breadth-oriented critique covering every enumerated rubric column paired with headline verdict language keyed to quantitative threshold bands spelled out operator-side.

cases.prompt: Depth study for `workspace/specs/20270412-cache-hardening`: hydrate the Markdown index spine, hydrate each chained planner subtask file, cross-verify claimed dependencies against codebase reality while keeping hands off deliverable code authored elsewhere, quantify the breadth rubric pillars at spec granularity plus dependency graph scepticism inside a Markdown report stored alongside the bundle using the dated assessment filename pattern the agent handbook prescribes for spec-scoped reviews, withhold every mechanical rewrite of authoritative planner prose until qualitative findings solidify entirely, escalate through structured JSON carrying `needs_user_input` when seeking operator approval for planned spec-plane edits instead of opening ad-hoc conversational approval prompts from the reviewer session.
follow_ups: Respond with affirmative structured acknowledgement authorising selective planner-only edits enumerated in reviewer guidance (index cadence headings, tightened acceptance prose, clarified dependency arcs); executables, automated suites, and configuration files must remain untouched. Ensure the reopened Markdown dossier cites what mechanical remediation shipped once typing concludes.
assertions: The qualitative Markdown dossier publishes prior to authoring any sanctioned planner artefacts; unsolicited structural edits touching indexes, authored subtasks, or dependency exposition wait behind structured escalation payloads rather than improvised chat prompts.

After explicit affirmative structured signalling, authoring stays limited strictly to sanctioned planner artefacts while executables, automated suites, configs, or builder-maintained prose outside that allowance remain untouched on disk.

The reopened assessment Markdown recounts mechanically applied remediation so operators can correlate narrative critique with ensuing planner deltas while refreshed dependency commentary still reflects all six doctrinal pillars from the commanding workflow.
cases.expected_output: Feature-scoped dossier precedes mechanically touching planner scaffolding; affirmative multi-turn signalling unlocks limited index and subtask rewrite scope while closing annotations describe every structural adjustment performed.

cases.prompt: zoto-spec-executor invoked you after subtask 02 closed: read specs/active-feature/subtasks/subta<token>.md together with specs/active-feature/status/subta<token>.status.yml, reconcile checklist.done against the workspace, merge extra.judge with verdict verified and an empty fix_list after independent filesystem checks, re-render the paired status markdown using spec-status-roundtrip md-from-yml only, run spec-onstop-check.ts --human for that spec directory and repo root, then report verified back to the executor.
assertions: After reconciliation, every checklist item marked done in specs/active-feature/status/subtask-02-auth-flow-20260507.status.yml matches evidence found on disk from the Deliverables Checklist and Definition of Done sections.

The agent merged verdict notes and extra.judge.fix_list into specs/active-feature/status/subtask-02-auth-flow-20260507.status.yml and regenerated specs/active-feature/status/subtask-02-auth-flow-20260507.status.md via spec-status-roundtrip md-from-yml rather than hand-editing markdown.
The agent ran plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-onstop-check.ts with --human before claiming verified and did not report verified if that process exited with status 2.
No writes occurred outside specs/active-feature/status/ for the subtask pair, and no tools attempted to modify the subtask markdown spec file or any deliverable paths referenced by the checklist.
Each status-pair YAML records the judge result under an `extra:` then `judge:` block (i.e. `extra.judge.verdict`, `extra.judge.fix_list`, etc.).
Verdict enum values are one of `verdict: verified`, `verdict: partial`, or `verdict: failed` per the documented spec-judge rubric.
Before signalling verified, the judge invokes `plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-onstop-check.ts --human` and treats exit code 2 as a hard blocker.
cases.expected_output: Only the subtask status YAML and its round-trip markdown change; extra.judge records verified with empty fix_list; spec-onstop-check exits zero; no edits land on application sources, tests, the subtask markdown spec, the spec index Status column, execution report, or spec-root status files.

primitive_analysis.summary: The judge agent verifies subtask completion through adversarial checks confined to each subtask status pair, gates verified outcomes with spec-onstop-check, and performs repo-wide or spec-scoped assessments via zoto-judge-spec while keeping deliverables untouched until Mode 3 user-approved spec edits.

cases.prompt: Executor handed you subtask 04: specs/active-feature/subtasks/subta<token>.md plus specs/active-feature/status/subta<token>.status.yml shows checklist items marked done but the throttle helper file is missing. Stay within Mode 1 reviewer constraints, downgrade reconciliation so unverified items remain false, populate extra.judge with verdict partial, timestamp, notes, and a fix_list entry whose recommended_path names the missing module without editing that path yourself, round-trip the status markdown from YAML, run spec-onstop-check.ts --human, then return partial to the executor.
assertions: extra.judge.verdict is partial (not verified) while any Deliverables Checklist or Definition of Done item lacks proof, and fix_list entries describe concrete gaps with severity and recommended_path suitable for executor routing.
The agent never patched application code, configs, tests, documentation deliverables, or the subtask markdown spec to remediate the gap — only the status pair files were written.
spec-status-roundtrip md-from-yml was used after YAML edits so markdown mirrors YAML rather than diverging.
If spec-onstop-check exits 2 because verdict or state contradicts open checklist items, the reported verdict and notes acknowledge partial or failed rather than insisting on verified.
Each status-pair YAML records the judge result under an `extra:` then `judge:` block (i.e. `extra.judge.verdict`, `extra.judge.fix_list`, etc.).
Verdict enum values are one of `verdict: verified`, `verdict: partial`, or `verdict: failed` per the documented spec-judge rubric.
Before signalling verified, the judge invokes `plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-onstop-check.ts --human` and treats exit code 2 as a hard blocker.
cases.expected_output: Status YAML shows checklist.done false for items lacking proof; extra.judge.verdict is partial with at least one structured fix_list row carrying severity and recommended_path for routing to the originally assigned subagent; round-trip keeps YAML canonical; onStop reflects the inconsistency if state or verdict disagrees with open items.

primitive_analysis.summary: The judge agent verifies subtask completion through adversarial checks confined to each subtask status pair, gates verified outcomes with spec-onstop-check, and performs repo-wide or spec-scoped assessments via zoto-judge-spec while keeping deliverables untouched until Mode 3 user-approved spec edits.

cases.prompt: Run /z-spec-judge with no arguments after loading .zoto/spec-system/config.yml so specsDir resolves correctly. Use the zoto-judge-spec skill to examine the repository structure, perform read-only stack-specific sanity checks, score Completeness Feasibility Structure Specificity Risk Awareness and Convention Compliance, choose an Approve Conditional or Reject verdict using the documented thresholds, and write specs/assessment-repo-20260507.md with those scores and rationale.
assertions: The agent invoked /z-spec-judge without a trailing spec path and relied on zoto-judge-spec workflow steps covering breadth of the tree plus tailored read-only checks.
The written report includes scores for Completeness, Feasibility, Structure, Specificity, Risk Awareness, and Convention Compliance even when dimensions look healthy.
Filesystem mutations are limited to the assessment markdown under specsDir; no edits touched subtask deliverables or unrelated configuration.
The qualitative verdict (Approve at least 4.0 average, Conditional between 3.0 and 3.9, Reject below 3.0) aligns with the numeric averages stated in the report.
Each status-pair YAML records the judge result under an `extra:` then `judge:` block (i.e. `extra.judge.verdict`, `extra.judge.fix_list`, etc.).
Verdict enum values are one of `verdict: verified`, `verdict: partial`, or `verdict: failed` per the documented spec-judge rubric.
Before signalling verified, the judge invokes `plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-onstop-check.ts --human` and treats exit code 2 as a hard blocker.
cases.expected_output: A repo assessment markdown appears under the configured specs directory using the assessment-repo naming convention with the run date embedded in the filename, listing numeric scores for all six dimensions plus rationale tied to the documented verdict thresholds, without mutating application sources beyond that report.

primitive_analysis.summary: The judge agent verifies subtask completion through adversarial checks confined to each subtask status pair, gates verified outcomes with spec-onstop-check, and performs repo-wide or spec-scoped assessments via zoto-judge-spec while keeping deliverables untouched until Mode 3 user-approved spec edits.

cases.prompt: Run /z-spec-judge targeting specs/active-feature/spec-payment-hooks-20260507.md: load the spec index and subtasks, validate manifest dependencies using zoto-judge-spec, score all six dimensions, write specs/active-feature/assessment-payment-hooks-20260507.md capturing unbiased findings first, then surface actionable recommendations and explicitly ask whether I want spec markdown edits applied.
follow_ups: Yes — apply the approved wording tweaks only to the spec index and subtask markdown files you identified; skip application code entirely and annotate the assessment report with what changed.
No — leave every spec file byte-for-byte unchanged after publishing the assessment.
assertions: The agent produced specs/active-feature/assessment-payment-hooks-20260507.md inside the spec directory with complete scoring, manifest review, and dependency commentary prior to offering edits.
Until the user explicitly approves changes, the agent did not modify spec index files, subtask markdown specs, or dependency graph artifacts.
After explicit approval in the follow-up turn, updates touched only spec-facing markdown described in the plan and never altered application sources, configs, or tests owned by subagents.
When the user declines fixes in the follow-up turn, spec documents remained unchanged while the assessment still documents findings and verdict thresholds.
Each status-pair YAML records the judge result under an `extra:` then `judge:` block (i.e. `extra.judge.verdict`, `extra.judge.fix_list`, etc.).
Verdict enum values are one of `verdict: verified`, `verdict: partial`, or `verdict: failed` per the documented spec-judge rubric.
Before signalling verified, the judge invokes `plugins/zoto-spec-system/scripts/spec-onstop-check.ts --human` and treats exit code 2 as a hard blocker.
cases.expected_output: Before any approval message, the assessment markdown exists with full six-dimension scoring and risk discussion without premature spec edits; after approval the agent may revise only spec-level documents noted in the plan while leaving runtime code alone; after refusal only the assessment file reflects recommendations.

primitive_analysis.summary: The judge agent verifies subtask completion through adversarial checks confined to each subtask status pair, gates verified outcomes with spec-onstop-check, and performs repo-wide or spec-scoped assessments via zoto-judge-spec while keeping deliverables untouched until Mode 3 user-approved spec edits.
